evals.prompt: In this TypeScript API service, implement a POST /uploads endpoint that creates a presigned object-storage upload URL, validates max file size and MIME type, stores an upload intent row, and emits enough logs for debugging production upload failures.
evals.expected_output: Implementation plan or code changes should route through API, upload/media, security, observability, and resilience guidance; validate inputs server-side; keep object storage private; use short-lived scoped signed URLs; add tests for success and invalid inputs; avoid UI-only work.
expectations: Reads or applies references/api-standards.md, references/file-uploads-media.md, references/security-secrets.md, and references/observability.md.
Traces active API route, persistence, storage config, and auth boundary before editing.
Defines validation for MIME type, file size, ownership, signed URL scope, and error envelope.
Includes repository-native validation commands or tests.
Does not make buckets public or store secrets in plaintext.

evals.prompt: Review a Pulumi diff that adds CloudFront in front of our media bucket and opens a new origin access path. Tell me whether this is ready to deploy to production.
evals.expected_output: Review should produce severity-ordered findings and a GO/NO GO verdict based on infrastructure, networking/cache, security, observability, and production readiness evidence.
expectations: Reads or applies references/infrastructure.md, references/networking-caching.md, references/security-secrets.md, and references/production-readiness.md.
Checks public exposure, origin access, cache keys, TTLs, CORS, logging, IAM, and replacement/deletion risk.
Requires preview/plan evidence before deploy approval.
Does not approve production deploy solely because tests pass.

evals.prompt: Move invoice PDF generation out of the checkout request path into a background worker. The user should see status, failures should retry safely, and support needs enough telemetry to debug stuck jobs.
evals.expected_output: Output should design or implement an async processing path with producer/consumer schema, idempotency, retries, DLQ or poison handling, status persistence, and observability.
expectations: Reads or applies references/background-processing.md, references/resilience-idempotency.md, and references/observability.md.
Defines job schema, idempotency key, retry/backoff, dead-letter behavior, and completion/status model.
Preserves checkout request contract or explicitly changes it with tests.
Does not move work async without user-visible status or operational visibility.

evals.prompt: We have a nightly cron container that scans accounts and expires stale sessions. Replace it with a managed scheduler and make sure missed runs, overlaps, and partial failures are handled.
evals.expected_output: Output should select a scheduler/runtime pattern, define missed-run and overlap semantics, checkpointing, idempotency, observability, and validation for the scheduled job logic separate from trigger wiring.
expectations: Reads or applies references/scheduling.md, references/background-processing.md, references/resilience-idempotency.md, and references/infrastructure.md.
Defines time zone, missed-run behavior, overlap lock, checkpoint, and rerun safety.
Keeps schedule source of truth in repository infrastructure or runtime config.
Does not rely on local process timers for production-critical recurring work unless repository evidence supports singleton behavior.

evals.prompt: Do an architecture review of this branch that introduces a Lambda webhook handler, an SQS queue, and a worker that calls a third-party billing API. Focus on production readiness, secrets, idempotency, and observability.
evals.expected_output: Review should trace the full webhook-to-queue-to-worker path, identify security and resilience risks, check secrets and IAM, verify retry/idempotency behavior, and give production-readiness findings with severity.
expectations: Reads or applies references/architecture.md, references/security-secrets.md, references/resilience-idempotency.md, references/observability.md, and references/production-readiness.md.
Checks webhook validation, queue schema, retry semantics, duplicate delivery, secret handling, IAM scope, and alerting.
Reports findings before summaries and avoids generic best-practice prose without code/config evidence.
Does not assume SQS/Lambda semantics without verifying configured behavior.

evals.prompt: Compare whether we should keep our self-hosted image proxy or move to managed cloud services. Include replacement-cost categories and what evidence you need before recommending a path.
evals.expected_output: Output should use capability-first cloud equivalency, compare managed and self-hosted cost categories, list operational risks, require current provider pricing for numeric estimates, and avoid provider-specific conclusions without evidence.
expectations: Reads or applies references/cost-cloud-equivalency.md, references/file-uploads-media.md, and assets/cloud-mapping-matrix.md.
Compares service charges, adjacent services, egress, observability, engineering operations, migration, lock-in, and risk.
States assumptions and required current pricing evidence.
Does not present static prices from memory as current.
